description: Viewport-bound application frame using CSS Grid. Provides named layout slots: header (brand + nav + profile), aside (sidebar navigation), main (primary content), and footer. Ideal for dashboards, admin panels, and single-page apps.
emmet: div[data-appshell]>(header>(div[data-flex data-align=center data-gap=sm]>div{Logo}+nav>a{Home}+a{Docs})+(div[data-flex data-align=center]{User}))+(aside>nav[data-stack=xs]>a{Dashboard}+a{Reports}+a{Settings})+(main[data-stack=md]>h1{Page Title}+p{Main content area})+(footer>span{© 2026 Company})

data-appshell.agent_hint: Activates the full application shell grid layout. Bare attribute, no value needed.

--ix-appshell-sidebar-width.agent_hint: CSS custom property to control the sidebar width. Set as an inline style or in your stylesheet.

header.agent_hint: Top header bar. Use data-flex children to place brand logo (left), nav links (center), and user profile avatar (right).

sidebar.agent_hint: Left navigation sidebar. Use data-stack=xs inside for vertical nav links. Auto-stacks below header on mobile.

main.agent_hint: Primary scrollable content area. Use data-stack=md inside for page layout.

footer.agent_hint: Bottom footer bar spanning the full width. Typically contains copyright or secondary links.

do: Use header > div[data-flex] children for brand, nav, and profile slots
Use aside > nav[data-stack=xs] for vertical sidebar navigation
Use main as the primary scrollable content region
Use --ix-appshell-sidebar-width CSS variable to customize sidebar width
Set overflow: hidden on the root element when embedding in a fixed-height container
dont: Do not manually set grid-template-areas or grid-template-columns
Do not place content directly inside data-appshell without the semantic slot elements
Do not use data-appshell for scrolling document pages — use data-header-layout instead
